audit_score_rationale: Page mirrors the sibling Alabama wage-overtime-and-meal-break-law exemplar's structure precisely (masthead → breadcrumb → eyebrow → partner-badge → h1 → subhead → last-reviewed → G2 row → CTA → hero → chip-nav → capsule → 8 Q sections → pullquote → tagline-close → links-rail → meta-footer → customer-strip). Alaska's three structural surprises (daily 8-hour OT, no tip credit, AS 23.10.350 minor breaks) each get a dedicated section; the small-employer (<4) exemption and voluntary flex-plan get explicit treatment inside the overtime section. Inline stamp-block visual reinforces the AS 23.10.350 minor break rule. Hero photo from Unsplash (Simon Hurry, downtown Anchorage night) 207KB. All facts cross-checked against Alaska DOL primary sources (whact.htm, whfaq.htm) and statute text (AS 23.10.060, AS 23.10.350). One mid-year scheduled minimum wage jump (1 Jul 2026) noted in the page and flagged for re-check.

high_concern_flags.note: Alaska minimum wage jumps from $13.00 to $14.00 on 1 July 2026 and again to $15.00 on 1 July 2027 per Ballot Measure 1. The capsule, minimum-wage section, and tipped-wages section all reference both the 1 Jan 2026 and 1 Jul 2026 figures. Re-check after 1 July 2026 to confirm the second-half rate landed as scheduled (no court challenge or legislative repeal observed at draft time). Indexing to Anchorage CPI begins 1 January 2028 — first CPI-set rate will need to be sourced from Alaska DOL announcement in late 2027.

retrofit_needed.notes: United States parent and Alaska state parent are wave-3/wave-4 — link-gate has rendered them as plain text. Chip-nav references sibling Alaska pages (workers-compensation, new-hire-reporting, employer-registrations, paid-leave-and-pto, termination-at-will, hiring-guide) that ship in wave-4. The existing Alaska state-tax sibling uses 'minimum-wage-and-overtime' in its chip-nav; this page uses 'wage-overtime-and-meal-break-law' per the row spec — a retrofit to align chip-nav across all Alaska sibling pages is queued for wave-4 cleanup.

compliance_cache_reused: sprint/compliance-cache/united-states.json (2026-05-20) — used for federal baseline only (FLSA $7.25, $684 salary basis, FUTA, PUMP Act). Alaska-specific facts verified via WebSearch against Alaska DOL primary sources on 2026-05-21.
page_specific_notes: Page leads with the multi-pronged employer surprise — Alaska is one of only two US states with mandatory daily overtime (with California), and one of only seven states that prohibit the FLSA tip credit entirely
The 4-employee small-employer exemption from AS 23.10.060(d)(5) is given explicit treatment because most early-stage US clients sit inside it on day one
Voluntary flexible work-hour plan (AS 23.10.060(15); 8 AAC 15.102) framed as the practical pressure valve for compressed schedules; Teamed handles the DOL filing
AS 23.10.350 minor break rule treated with the stamp-block visual — single Alaska-only statutory addition above federal floor on the breaks side
Multi-state-employer section explicitly contrasts Alaska's daily OT + no tip credit floor with California's meal/rest break premiums and federal-floor states
Cost-page conditional rule satisfied via 'passes through at cost, itemised on every invoice' anchor in how-teamed-handles section
Cross-state sibling link to Alabama wage-overtime-and-meal-break-law added in links-rail to seed wave-2 US state cross-linking
Chip-nav uses 'wage-overtime-and-meal-break-law' per row slug; existing Alaska state-tax sibling chip-nav uses 'minimum-wage-and-overtime' — alignment retrofit queued
